---
title: "ABS Fire"
author: "Solomon Maerowitz-McMahan"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: word_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r process-and-display, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(flextable)

# Read data
Nute_table <- read.csv('Nute_table.csv')

# Process the data
Nute_table_processed <- Nute_table %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., digits = 2))) %>%
  mutate(Site = factor(Site, levels = c(5, 7, 8, 10, 11, 12, 26, 29, 31, 34, 49, 56))) %>%
  arrange(Site)

# Rename columns for clarity
Nute_table_processed <- Nute_table_processed %>%
  rename(PO4 = PO4_Resin, NH4_Resin = NH4_Resin, NO3_Resin = NO3_Resin)

# Create a flextable for Word output
Nute_table_flex <- Nute_table_processed %>%
  select(Site, PO4, NH4_Resin, NO3_Resin, Bray.P, NH4, NO3, Total.P, Carbon) %>%
  flextable() %>%
  set_header_labels(
    PO4 = "PO4", NH4_Resin = "NH4", NO3_Resin = "NO3", 
    Bray.P = "Bray P", NH4 = "NH4", NO3 = "NO3", 
    Total.P = "Total P", Carbon = "Carbon"
  ) %>%
  add_header_row(
    values = c("", "", "Resins mg/kg", "", "", "", "Soil mg/kg", "", ""),
    colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1)
  ) %>%
  theme_vanilla()
Nute_table_flex
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
